name: BFS
notes: Complete and optimal on unweighted grids
reference: Cormen et al.; classical graph traversal

name: DFS
notes: Complete on finite grids but not shortest-path optimal

name: Uniform-Cost Search
notes: Dijkstra-style cheapest-first search

name: Dijkstra
notes: Shortest paths with non-negative edge weights

name: A*
notes: Optimal with admissible consistent heuristic

name: Greedy Best-First
notes: Heuristic-only priority, not optimal

name: Bidirectional BFS
notes: Two unweighted waves meet in the middle

name: IDDFS
notes: Memory-light complete depth search

name: IDA*
notes: A* contours with DFS memory use

name: Bellman-Ford
notes: Repeated edge relaxation baseline

name: SPFA
notes: Queue-optimized Bellman-Ford variant

name: Lee
notes: Grid-routing BFS distance wave

name: Flood Fill
notes: Goal-distance field used in micromouse routing

name: Hadlock
notes: 0-1 detour-number routing on rectilinear grids

name: Dead-End Filling
notes: Prunes cul-de-sacs before tracing remaining corridor

name: Left-Hand Rule
notes: Topology-dependent local wall follower

name: Right-Hand Rule
notes: Mirrored topology-dependent wall follower

name: Tremaux
notes: Marks passages to avoid endless loops

name: Pledge
notes: Preferred heading with turn-sum obstacle escape

name: Random Mouse
notes: Random-walk baseline with probabilistic hitting time

name: Dial's Algorithm
notes: Integer-weight Dijkstra using buckets

name: 0-1 BFS
notes: Deque shortest path for 0/1 edge weights

name: Theta*
notes: Any-angle paths with line-of-sight relaxation

name: Lazy Theta*
notes: Fewer line-of-sight checks than Theta*

name: Field D*
notes: Interpolation-based continuous-cost grid replanning

name: Jump Point Search
notes: Symmetry pruning for uniform-cost grids

name: JPS+
notes: Preprocessed jump points for faster grid search

name: Rectangular Symmetry Reduction
notes: Prunes rectangular grid symmetries

name: Fringe Search
notes: Batch-oriented A* alternative for games

name: HPA*
notes: Clustered abstraction for large maps

name: D*
notes: Dynamic path repair for changing maps

name: D* Lite
notes: LPA*-based replanning for unknown terrain

name: LPA*
notes: Reuses prior search after graph-cost changes

name: Anytime Repairing A*
notes: Improves paths as time allows

name: Weighted A*
notes: Inflates heuristic for speed at bounded quality

name: Beam Search
notes: Keeps only best frontier width, incomplete generally

name: Hill Climbing
notes: Greedy local improvement, trap-prone

name: Bug 1
notes: Boundary-following robot navigation baseline

name: Bug 2
notes: M-line obstacle navigation baseline

name: TangentBug
notes: Range-sensor local tangent navigation

name: Potential Field
notes: Gradient descent over attractive/repulsive field

name: Fast Marching Method
notes: Eikonal front propagation over grids

name: Fast Sweeping Method
notes: Eikonal solver via directional sweeps

name: Value Iteration
notes: MDP/grid cost-to-go dynamic programming

name: Policy Iteration
notes: Iterates policy evaluation and improvement

name: RRT
notes: Rapidly-exploring random trees for continuous space

name: RRT*
notes: Asymptotically optimal RRT variant

name: PRM
notes: Roadmap sampling for repeated queries

name: Voronoi Roadmap
notes: Obstacle-clearance skeleton navigation

name: Navigation Mesh A*
notes: A* over convex navigation polygons

name: Contraction Hierarchies
notes: Node-ordering speedup for shortest paths

name: ALT / A* Landmarks
notes: Landmark triangle-inequality heuristics

name: Hierarchical Dijkstra
notes: Multi-level graph abstraction

name: Floyd-Warshall
notes: All-pairs dynamic programming baseline for small maze graphs

name: Johnson's Algorithm
notes: Sparse all-pairs shortest paths via reweighting and repeated Dijkstra

name: DAG Shortest Path
notes: Topological relaxation for acyclic passage graphs or reduced corridor DAGs
reference: Classical shortest paths in directed acyclic graphs

name: Multi-Source BFS
notes: Several simultaneous sources produce nearest-source distance fields

name: Reverse BFS
notes: Runs from the goal to expose predecessor gradients for every start

name: Brushfire Distance Transform
notes: Obstacle or goal wavefront labels cells by discrete distance

name: Perimeter Search
notes: Precomputes a search perimeter near the goal for later forward search

name: Recursive Best-First Search
notes: A* style best-first behavior with linear memory recursion

name: SMA*
notes: Simplified memory-bounded A* retains the best frontier allowed by memory

name: MM Bidirectional Search
notes: Meets in the middle with heuristic guarantees about expansion depth
reference: Holte et al. MM bidirectional heuristic search

name: Near-Optimal Bidirectional Search
notes: Chooses front-to-front expansions to approach minimum necessary work

name: Front-to-Front Bidirectional A*
notes: Heuristic estimates are evaluated between opposite frontier nodes

name: ANYA
notes: Interval-based optimal any-angle search on grids

name: Block A*
notes: Uses precomputed local block distances to accelerate grid A*

name: Subgoal Graphs
notes: Precomputed turning subgoals shorten online grid search

name: Swamps Pruning
notes: Identifies regions irrelevant to particular start-goal pairs

name: Corridor Graph Reduction
notes: Contracts degree-two corridor chains into weighted junction edges
reference: Classical graph contraction for maze and road networks

name: Junction Graph Search
notes: Searches only junctions, endpoints, and corridor segments

name: Ariadne's Thread
notes: Backtracking path-marking method related to Tremaux-style navigation

name: Medial-Axis Routing
notes: Routes over a skeleton that maximizes obstacle clearance

name: Dynamic Window Approach
notes: Samples feasible robot velocities for local collision-free progress
reference: Fox, Burgard, Thrun dynamic window approach

name: Hybrid A*
notes: A* over discretized pose states for nonholonomic motion
reference: Dolgov et al. practical search techniques

name: Lattice Planner
notes: Searches a motion-primitive lattice embedded in the plane

name: BIT*
notes: Batch informed tree search for asymptotically optimal planning

name: Informed RRT*
notes: Restricts RRT* sampling to an improving informed subset after a solution

name: Ant Colony Optimization
notes: Pheromone-guided stochastic route optimization over maze edges

name: Genetic Algorithm
notes: Evolves encoded candidate paths with selection and mutation

name: Simulated Annealing
notes: Stochastic local search accepts occasional worse moves while cooling

name: Tabu Search
notes: Local search with short-term memory to avoid cycling

name: Q-Learning Grid Solver
notes: Learns state-action values for grid navigation rewards

name: SAT Path Encoding
notes: Encodes bounded path existence as Boolean satisfiability

name: Integer Linear Programming
notes: Solves maze paths as flow or shortest-path linear constraints
